title: Sanji’s Childhood Wound
description: On pages 6–9, this part focuses on Unknown person #5, Unknown person #6, Unknown person #7, Judge, Unknown person #8, Sanji as Sanji’s childhood trauma and the political trap around his marriage collide with Luffy’s refusal to abandon him.
scene: Sanji’s past explains why his family name is a wound rather than a source of pride.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Coming all this way just to see me! Oh, Sanji...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You'll get a stern talking to from your father again if he finds out!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Mmmmm.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Don't tell me... Did you prepare this dish for me?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Y... Yeah...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Epony!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Y... YES MA'AM!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You no longer need waste your time and efforts preparing these meals... I will simply dine on whatever it is that Sanji has made for me!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Ah... well... what he made had gotten rather ...scrambled during his journey here...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: So we had no choice but to dispose of it in the trash!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: How lovely!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: egg rolls... fish and... some bananas, it looks like.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: I... I'm sorry, Mother! I messed up!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I had a little incident on the way here and dropped it...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Then it got crushed...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And it's wet from the rain too... and...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Well, let's see...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: MMMMM! This tastes great!!()

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Really- yy?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Will you... make some- thing for me again?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: A cookbook and some cooking utensils?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: The all... Blue?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Hey, look... it really is him.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Man, you're right!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: So you didn't really kick the bucket!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: for father to go through that entire charade...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Well duh... It's because he wished the lie were true!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Then maybe we can bring Father True Happiness...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: By making sure you really do die!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Remember to see if

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: you're a friggin...

dialogue.speaker: Judge
dialogue.text: Total failure!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Just to clarify, I'm not on your side, ok?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I don't want to end up as their next target.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Those three may have turned out to be the ultimate warriors father dreamed of...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But they're utterly Inca- Pable of feeling emotions like empathy or sympathy!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: "Modifications" were successful on my body as well... but I'm nothing like them.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Just so you know... we're about to climb the red line.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: apparently we're going to be fighting some wars in the East Blue.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: We will begin our journey...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Towards the East Blue!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The ETA will be approximately 3 weeks from now.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Reju...

summary: This section advances the Sanji and the Vinsmokes storyline with 43 dialogue beats. Sanji’s family trauma and the forced wedding turn the rescue mission into an emotional test of loyalty.
